LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. THE MATTER WAS ARGUED AS TO THE 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DENY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. DENY MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION (RACE DISCRIMINATION). GRANT MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO REMAINING CAUSES OF ACTION (1, 2, 3, 5, 6 AND 7). #1 - (WAGE DISCRIMINATION) NO EVIDENCE THAT ANY DISPARITY IN WAGE BASED RACE; #2 - RETALIATION - NO EVIDENCE OF RETALIATION BY FRITZ/DEFENDANT; #3 - FAILURE PREVENT HARASSMENT - NO EVIDENCE OF HARASSMENT (DIRTY LOOK, WHAT NEED TO DO GET PROMOTION BE BLACK) NEED SEVERE AND PERVASIVE CONDUCT THAT ALTERS WORK CONDITIONS; #5 - FAILURE TO ACCOMMODATE - NOT MENTIONED IN EEOC 1-3 CHARGES FAILED EXHAUST ADMINISTRATIVE REMEDIES TITLE VII; #6 (RACE DISCRIMINATION) AND #7 (RETALIATION) - TIME BARRED BY 90 DAYS. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. THE COURT WILL RULE ON THE PARTIES' OBJECTIONS AFTER THE HEARING. JUDGE: PAUL H. ALVARADO, COURT REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-15-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
Appearances: Karen E. Giquinto of Lombardi Loper & Conant, LLP (510.433.2600) for defendant and moving party; Curtis E. Allen (650.868.6620) and Pamela Y. Price of Price and Associates (510.452.0292) for plaintiff and responding party. The matter was argued as to the 4th cause of action. The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: deny motion for summary judgment. Deny motion for summary adjudication as to 4th cause of action (race discrimination). Grant motion for summary adjudication as to remaining causes of action (1, 2, 3, 5, 6 and 7). #1 - (wage discrimination) no evidence that any disparity in wage based race; #2 - retaliation - no evidence of retaliation by Fritz/defendant; #3 - failure prevent harassment - no evidence of harassment (dirty look, what need to do get promotion be black) need severe and pervasive conduct that alters work conditions; #5 - failure to accommodate - not mentioned in EEOC 1-3 charges failed exhaust administrative remedies Title VII; #6 (race discrimination) and #7 (retaliation) - time barred by 90 days. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. The court will rule on the parties' objections after the hearing. (302/PHA) Judge: Paul H. Alvarado, Court Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
